response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she uses 1/2 ounce every day. Over the course of a leap year, which has 366 days, she will use 1/2 ounce per day × 366 days = 183 ounces of shampoo. Since she buys shampoo in 14-ounce bottles, she will need to buy [number_of_bottles] bottles to cover her needs. Each bottle contains 14 ounces, so the number of bottles needed is calculated by dividing the total ounces by the size of each bottle: 183 ÷ 14 = 13.071... which means she needs 14 bottles to have enough shampoo for the entire leap year.

[address] is where she can find the 14-ounce bottles of shampoo. She should check the price and availability at the store to ensure she gets the best deal. With 14 bottles, she will have enough shampoo for the entire leap year, and she will have [remaining_ounces] ounces left over.
